options.text: Deploy shared AVD security infrastructure: a Secrets Key Vault for storing AVD credentials, and an Encryption Key Vault for Customer-Managed Key (CMK) encryption. Run this deployment before deploying host pools, image management, or add-ons that use CMK. The resource IDs output by this deployment are used as inputs to downstream solutions.

elements.name: nameConvResTypeAtEnd
elements.label: Resource Type Abbreviation at End of Name
elements.toolTip: When checked, names follow the pattern 'avd-security-eus-rg'. When unchecked (default), names follow 'rg-avd-security-eus'.

options.text: The Secrets Key Vault (Standard SKU) stores AVD credentials: VM local administrator and domain join account. Downstream deployers reference this vault by resource ID only — they do not need read access to the secrets themselves. To read secrets at deployment time (e.g., for getSecret() ARM references), the deploying identity needs the 'Key Vault Secrets User' role.

elements.name: deploySecretsKeyVault
elements.label: Deploy Secrets Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Deploy a Standard SKU Key Vault for storing AVD credentials.

elements.name: enableSoftDelete
elements.label: Enable Soft Delete
elements.toolTip: Allow recovery of deleted Key Vault objects within the retention period.

elements.name: enablePurgeProtection
elements.label: Enable Purge Protection
elements.toolTip: Prevent permanent deletion of the Key Vault and its objects during the retention period. Strongly recommended for production environments.

elements.name: retentionDays
elements.label: Soft Delete Retention (Days)
elements.toolTip: Number of days to retain soft-deleted Key Vault objects before permanent deletion.

options.text: Provide the AVD session host credentials to store in the Secrets Key Vault. All fields are optional — you can store credentials later by re-running this deployment. Domain join credentials are only required for ADDS or Entra Kerberos hybrid environments.

elements.name: virtualMachineAdminUserName
elements.label: Admin Username
elements.toolTip: Local administrator username for AVD session hosts. Stored as 'VirtualMachineAdminUserName' in the Key Vault.

constraints.validationMessage: Must start with a letter. Alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens allowed. Maximum 20 characters.

elements.name: virtualMachineAdminPassword
elements.toolTip: Local administrator password for AVD session hosts. Stored as 'VirtualMachineAdminPassword' in the Key Vault. Must meet Windows password complexity requirements.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 12-123 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, a digit, and a special character.

elements.name: domainJoinUserPrincipalName
elements.label: Domain Join UPN
elements.toolTip: UPN of the privileged account used to domain join session hosts. Stored as 'DomainJoinUserPrincipalName' in the Key Vault.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid UPN format (user@domain.tld).

elements.name: domainJoinUserPassword
elements.toolTip: Password for the domain join account. Stored as 'DomainJoinUserPassword' in the Key Vault.

options.text: The Encryption Key Vault (Premium SKU) stores RSA-4096 keys for Customer-Managed Key (CMK) encryption of VM disks, FSLogix storage, image management storage, image build storage, and function app storage. Required when any AVD solution uses CMK. The deploying identity requires the 'Key Vault Crypto Officer' role on this vault at deployment time to create encryption keys.

elements.name: deployEncryptionKeyVault
elements.label: Deploy Encryption Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Deploy a Premium SKU Key Vault (HSM-backed) for Customer-Managed Key encryption across all AVD solutions.

elements.name: encKvRetentionInDays
elements.label: Encryption Key Vault Soft Delete Retention (Days)
elements.toolTip: Number of days the Encryption Key Vault and its objects are retained after deletion before permanent removal.

elements.name: forcePublicAccess
elements.label: Allow public network access on Encryption Key Vault
elements.toolTip: When checked, the Encryption Key Vault is deployed with public network access enabled. Required when this vault will be used for Customer-Managed Key encryption on a Recovery Services Vault with private endpoints, because Azure Backup does not support the AzureServices trusted service bypass.

options.text: Configure private endpoints to disable public network access on the Key Vaults and route all traffic over the Microsoft backbone. Requires an existing virtual network with a dedicated subnet (no delegations) and a Private DNS Zone for Key Vault (privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net).

elements.name: privateEndpoint
elements.label: Deploy Private Endpoints
elements.toolTip: When enabled, public network access is disabled on both Key Vaults and private endpoints are deployed into the selected subnet.

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network containing the private endpoint subnet. Only virtual networks in the selected region are shown.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('networking').networkSection.virtualNetworksApi.value, (vnet) => equals(vnet.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (vnet) => parse(concat('{"label":"', vnet.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(vnet.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"name":"', vnet.name, '","id":"', vnet.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: privateEndpointSubnet
elements.label: Private Endpoint Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select the subnet for Key Vault private endpoints. Must not have any service delegations.

elements.name: enablePrivateDNSIntegration
elements.label: Enable Private DNS Zone Integration
elements.toolTip: Automatically register Key Vault private endpoints with an Azure Private DNS Zone for proper name resolution.

elements.name: privateDNSZonesSubscription
elements.label: Private DNS Zone Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription that contains your Private DNS Zones. In hub-and-spoke topologies this is typically the hub/connectivity subscription.

elements.name: keyVaultPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Key Vault Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the Private DNS Zone for Key Vault (privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net or privatelink.vaultcore.usgovcloudapi.net for Azure Government).

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('networking').privateDnsSection.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (zone) => contains(zone.name, 'vaultcore')), (zone) => parse(concat('{"label":"', zone.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(zone.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', zone.id, '"}')))]

options.text: Restrict public access to the Key Vaults to specific IP addresses or CIDR ranges. Use this when managing the deployment from a trusted workstation outside the Azure network boundary. Leave empty to allow all public traffic (or rely solely on private endpoints if deployed).

elements.name: permittedIPs
elements.ariaLabel: Permitted IP Addresses or CIDR Ranges
elements.label: Permitted IP Addresses / CIDR Ranges

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid IPv4 address or CIDR range.

options.text: Optionally send Key Vault audit and diagnostic logs to an existing Log Analytics Workspace. Key Vault logs capture secret access, key operations, and access control changes — security and compliance telemetry best suited for a security-team-owned workspace or SIEM.

elements.name: enableMonitoring
elements.label: Send Key Vault Diagnostic Logs to Log Analytics

elements.name: monitoringSubscription
elements.label: Log Analytics Workspace Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing the target Log Analytics Workspace.

elements.name: logAnalyticsWorkspace
elements.label: Log Analytics Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select the Log Analytics Workspace for Key Vault diagnostic logs.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('monitoring').logAnalyticsWorkspacesApi.value, (la) => parse(concat('{"label":"', la.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(la.id, '/'), 4)), ' | Location: ', la.location, '","value":"', la.id, '"}')))]

options.text: Optionally define a custom naming convention by selecting up to 5 segments in the order you want them to appear in resource names. When enabled, the Cloud Adoption Framework naming convention (including the 'Resource Type at End' option on the Deployment Basics step) is ignored.

elements.name: enableCustomNaming
elements.label: Enable Custom Naming Convention
elements.toolTip: When checked, all key vault resources will be named according to the segments and order you define below.

options.text: Select the segments that make up your naming convention and arrange them in order using Segment 1 through Segment 5. Select 'None (stop here)' to end the sequence. Auto-resolved segments — Resource Type, Purpose, Location — are substituted per resource at deployment time. Value segments — Enterprise Group, Environment, Free-form Text — require you to supply a value in the fields that appear below.

elements.name: separator
elements.label: Separator
elements.toolTip: Character(s) placed between each segment. A hyphen (-) is most common. Note: Key Vault names allow only hyphens — underscores and dots are automatically replaced with hyphens in Key Vault names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 3 characters: hyphens (-), underscores (_), or dots (.) only.

elements.name: segment1
elements.label: Segment 1
elements.toolTip: First segment of every resource name.

allowedValues.label: Enterprise Group
allowedValues.description: Org-level workload prefix — provide a value below (e.g. dv, avd, corp).

allowedValues.label: Environment
allowedValues.description: Deployment environment tier — provide a value below (e.g. pr, np, co, dev).

allowedValues.label: Resource Type
allowedValues.description: Resource type abbreviation — auto-resolved per resource (e.g. rg, kv). Optionally customize abbreviations below.

allowedValues.label: Purpose
allowedValues.description: Resource purpose — auto-resolved per resource at deployment time (e.g. operations, sec, enc). No value required.

allowedValues.label: Location
allowedValues.description: Region abbreviation — auto-resolved from the deployment location (e.g. eus, va). Optionally override below.

allowedValues.label: Free-form Text
allowedValues.description: A fixed string you define — provide a value below. The same text appears in every resource name.

elements.name: segment2
elements.label: Segment 2
elements.toolTip: Second segment. Select 'None (stop here)' to use only 1 segment.

allowedValues.label: None (stop here)
allowedValues.description: End the convention after Segment 1.

allowedValues.label: Enterprise Group
allowedValues.description: Org-level workload prefix — provide a value below (e.g. dv, avd, corp).

allowedValues.label: Environment
allowedValues.description: Deployment environment tier — provide a value below (e.g. pr, np, co, dev).

allowedValues.label: Resource Type
allowedValues.description: Resource type abbreviation — auto-resolved per resource (e.g. rg, kv). Optionally customize abbreviations below.

allowedValues.label: Purpose
allowedValues.description: Resource purpose — auto-resolved per resource at deployment time (e.g. operations, sec, enc). No value required.

allowedValues.label: Location
allowedValues.description: Region abbreviation — auto-resolved from the deployment location (e.g. eus, va). Optionally override below.

allowedValues.label: Free-form Text
allowedValues.description: A fixed string you define — provide a value below. The same text appears in every resource name.

elements.name: segment3
elements.label: Segment 3
elements.toolTip: Third segment. Select 'None (stop here)' to use only 2 segments.

allowedValues.label: None (stop here)
allowedValues.description: End the convention after Segment 2.

allowedValues.label: Enterprise Group
allowedValues.description: Org-level workload prefix — provide a value below (e.g. dv, avd, corp).

allowedValues.label: Environment
allowedValues.description: Deployment environment tier — provide a value below (e.g. pr, np, co, dev).

allowedValues.label: Resource Type
allowedValues.description: Resource type abbreviation — auto-resolved per resource (e.g. rg, kv). Optionally customize abbreviations below.

allowedValues.label: Purpose
allowedValues.description: Resource purpose — auto-resolved per resource at deployment time (e.g. operations, sec, enc). No value required.

allowedValues.label: Location
allowedValues.description: Region abbreviation — auto-resolved from the deployment location (e.g. eus, va). Optionally override below.

allowedValues.label: Free-form Text
allowedValues.description: A fixed string you define — provide a value below. The same text appears in every resource name.

elements.name: segment4
elements.label: Segment 4
elements.toolTip: Fourth segment. Select 'None (stop here)' to use only 3 segments.

allowedValues.label: None (stop here)
allowedValues.description: End the convention after Segment 3.

allowedValues.label: Enterprise Group
allowedValues.description: Org-level workload prefix — provide a value below (e.g. dv, avd, corp).

allowedValues.label: Environment
allowedValues.description: Deployment environment tier — provide a value below (e.g. pr, np, co, dev).

allowedValues.label: Resource Type
allowedValues.description: Resource type abbreviation — auto-resolved per resource (e.g. rg, kv). Optionally customize abbreviations below.

allowedValues.label: Purpose
allowedValues.description: Resource purpose — auto-resolved per resource at deployment time (e.g. operations, sec, enc). No value required.

allowedValues.label: Location
allowedValues.description: Region abbreviation — auto-resolved from the deployment location (e.g. eus, va). Optionally override below.

allowedValues.label: Free-form Text
allowedValues.description: A fixed string you define — provide a value below. The same text appears in every resource name.

elements.name: segment5
elements.label: Segment 5
elements.toolTip: Fifth segment. Select 'None (stop here)' to use only 4 segments.

allowedValues.label: None (stop here)
allowedValues.description: End the convention after Segment 4.

allowedValues.label: Enterprise Group
allowedValues.description: Org-level workload prefix — provide a value below (e.g. dv, avd, corp).

allowedValues.label: Environment
allowedValues.description: Deployment environment tier — provide a value below (e.g. pr, np, co, dev).

allowedValues.label: Resource Type
allowedValues.description: Resource type abbreviation — auto-resolved per resource (e.g. rg, kv). Optionally customize abbreviations below.

allowedValues.label: Purpose
allowedValues.description: Resource purpose — auto-resolved per resource at deployment time (e.g. operations, sec, enc). No value required.

allowedValues.label: Location
allowedValues.description: Region abbreviation — auto-resolved from the deployment location (e.g. eus, va). Optionally override below.

allowedValues.label: Free-form Text
allowedValues.description: A fixed string you define — provide a value below. The same text appears in every resource name.

elements.name: enterpriseGroupValue
elements.label: Enterprise Group Value
elements.toolTip: The fixed value used for the Enterprise Group segment in all resource names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: environmentValue
elements.label: Environment Value
elements.placeholder: e.g. pr, np, co, dev, prod
elements.toolTip: The fixed value used for the Environment segment in all resource names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: freeformValue
elements.label: Free-form Text Value
elements.toolTip: A fixed text string embedded in every resource name when the Free-form Text segment is used.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 20 lowercase letters, digits, or hyphens.

elements.name: locationAbbreviationOverride
elements.label: Custom Location Abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Override the auto-resolved region abbreviation. Leave empty to use the standard abbreviation from the locations data file (e.g. eus for East US, va for US Gov Virginia).

constraints.validationMessage: Use up to 10 lowercase letters or digits. Leave empty to use the auto-resolved abbreviation.

options.text: No Location segment selected. If you plan to deploy Key Vaults in multiple regions, include Location in your convention to avoid resource name conflicts between deployments.

elements.name: customizeRtCodes
elements.label: Customize Resource Type Abbreviations
elements.toolTip: Override the default Cloud Adoption Framework resource type abbreviations. When unchecked, CAF defaults are used: rg, kv, pe.

elements.name: rtCodeRg
elements.label: Resource Groups abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type segment in resource group names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodeKv
elements.label: Key Vaults abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type segment in Key Vault names. Key Vault names allow only alphanumeric characters and hyphens.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodePe
elements.label: Private Endpoints abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type segment in private endpoint names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

options.text: Preview shows the resource group name. Key Vault names use the same convention with 'sec' and 'enc' as purpose tokens to distinguish the two vaults, are capped at 24 characters, and always include a 6-character unique suffix. Underscores and dots in the separator are replaced with hyphens in Key Vault names. Location shows '{loc}' when no custom abbreviation is provided — the actual value is resolved at deployment time.
Apply tags to the deployed resources. Tags are applied per resource type.
